description: Join Workout Button with Countdown Timer - Show a Join Workout button with live countdown on buddy/squad cards when a partner has an active workout within the 20-minute group workout window

userStories.title: Add shared constant and type for active partner workouts
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the GROUP_WORKOUT_WINDOW_MS constant and ActivePartnerWorkout type exported from @burnbuddy/shared so both the API and web app can use them.
acceptanceCriteria: GROUP_WORKOUT_WINDOW_MS (value: 20 * 60 * 1000 = 1200000) is exported from packages/shared/src/types.ts
GROUP_WORKOUT_WINDOW_MS is re-exported from packages/shared/src/index.ts
ActivePartnerWorkout interface exported from packages/shared/src/types.ts with fields: type ('buddy' | 'squad'), referenceId (string), earliestStartedAt (string — ISO 8601)
ActivePartnerWorkout is re-exported from packages/shared/src/index.ts
services/api/src/services/group-workout-detection.ts imports GROUP_WORKOUT_WINDOW_MS from @burnbuddy/shared instead of defining it locally
The local GROUP_WORKOUT_WINDOW_MS constant in group-workout-detection.ts is removed
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Small change: add constant + interface to shared types, update one import. Verify group-workout-detection.ts still compiles after the import change.

userStories.title: Add GET /workouts/partner-active API endpoint
userStories.description: As a frontend client, I want to fetch which of my burn buddies and squad members currently have active workouts within the group workout window, so I can display Join Workout buttons.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /workouts/partner-active endpoint exists in services/api/src/routes/workouts.ts
Endpoint is protected by requireAuth middleware
Response shape: { groupWorkoutWindowMs: number, activePartnerWorkouts: ActivePartnerWorkout[] }
For each burn buddy: includes an entry if the partner (not the current user) has an active workout started within the last 20 minutes
For each burn squad: includes an entry if ANY non-self member has an active workout within the last 20 minutes; earliestStartedAt is the earliest such workout's startedAt
Returns empty activePartnerWorkouts array when no partners have active workouts
Queries burn buddies using both uid1 and uid2 directions (matching pattern in group-workout-detection.ts)
Queries burn squads using memberUids array-contains
Uses getDb() from lib/firestore.ts for all Firestore access
Imports GROUP_WORKOUT_WINDOW_MS from @burnbuddy/shared
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Add the new route handler BEFORE the existing /:id routes to avoid Express route parameter conflicts. Follow the existing Firestore query pattern: simple single-field queries with in-memory filtering to avoid composite index requirements.

userStories.title: Add tests for partner-active endpoint
userStories.description: As a developer, I need automated tests for the GET /workouts/partner-active endpoint to prevent regressions.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests added to services/api/src/routes/workouts.test.ts in a new describe block
Test: returns empty array when no burn buddies or squads exist
Test: returns buddy entry when partner has active workout within 20-minute window
Test: excludes buddy entry when partner's workout started more than 20 minutes ago
Test: returns squad entry when a squad member has active workout within window
Test: returns correct earliestStartedAt for squad with multiple active members
Tests follow existing pattern: vi.hoisted + vi.mock + supertest + buildApp()
Mock setup includes burnBuddies and burnSquads collection query chains (add to existing hoisted mocks)
vi.resetAllMocks() in beforeEach resets new mocks
All tests pass (cd services/api && yarn test)
userStories.notes: Follow the exact mock pattern in the existing workouts.test.ts file. The hoisted mocks need new mock functions for burnBuddies and burnSquads collection queries. Each test should set up mock return values for the relevant collections.

userStories.title: Fetch active partner workouts and poll on home page
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the home page to fetch active partner workout data on load and poll every 30 seconds so the UI can display Join Workout buttons promptly.
acceptanceCriteria: loadData() in apps/web/src/app/page.tsx includes a call to GET /workouts/partner-active
groupWorkoutWindowMs stored in component state (number)
A map of referenceId to earliestStartedAt stored in component state
CombinedItem interface extended with optional activePartnerStartedAt field (string | undefined)
Each buddy/squad item is populated with activePartnerStartedAt from the map during item construction
Failure to fetch partner-active data does not block page load (uses .catch() fallback with empty defaults)
A useEffect polls GET /workouts/partner-active every 30 seconds and updates the activePartnerStartedAt on existing items
Polling stops on component unmount (clearInterval in cleanup)
Polling does not run while the user has an active workout
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Add the partner-active fetch to the existing Promise.all in loadData(). For polling, add a separate useEffect with setInterval(30000). When poll returns new data, update items state by merging activePartnerStartedAt values.

userStories.title: Render Join Workout button with countdown on buddy/squad cards
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a Join Workout button with a live countdown timer on each buddy/squad card where a partner is currently working out, so I can quickly join and have it count as a group workout.
acceptanceCriteria: Each buddy/squad card shows a Join Workout button when activePartnerStartedAt is set and the user does NOT have an active workout
A live countdown displayed in MM:SS format, calculated as (earliestStartedAt + groupWorkoutWindowMs) - Date.now()
Countdown updates every second via useEffect with setInterval(1000)
When countdown is under 5 minutes (300000ms), the countdown text has class text-red-600 (red color)
When countdown is above 5 minutes, countdown text has class text-gray-500 (normal color)
When countdown reaches 0 or below, the Join Workout button and countdown are hidden for that card
Button is NOT shown when the user already has an active workout (activeWorkout state is non-null)
Button appears below the existing last-group-workout and next-workout text, on the left side of the card
Button uses Tailwind classes for prominent green styling: bg-green-500 text-white rounded px-3 py-1.5 text-[13px] font-bold hover:bg-green-600
Clicking the Join Workout button opens the existing workout type selector modal (sets showWorkoutSelector to true)
After user selects a type and starts the workout, page data refreshes and Join buttons disappear
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: The countdown timer needs its own useEffect with a 1-second interval. Track countdown values in a state object keyed by item id. The Join Workout click handler should just call setShowWorkoutSelector(true) — the existing handleStartWorkout function already handles POST /workouts and refreshing state. Make sure to prevent the Link navigation when clicking the Join button (e.stopPropagation + e.preventDefault).

userStories.title: Push changes, create PR, and merge
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all changes pushed to the remote branch with a PR created and merged so the feature is delivered to main.
acceptanceCriteria: All changes committed and pushed to ralph/join-workout-button branch
Pull request created against main with a descriptive title and summary
PR description lists all user stories completed (US-001 through US-005)
All CI checks pass on the PR
PR is merged into main
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Final story. Push the branch, create a PR using gh CLI, wait for CI checks to pass, then merge. Use 'gh pr create' and 'gh pr merge' commands.
